_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
source_files: Ilizarov Fixation-Limb Lengthening-Bone Transport by Ilizarov_Medco.pdf
Ilizarov Fixation-Limb Lengthening-Bone Transport by Ilizarov_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: The Ilizarov technique can be used to correct deformities to bone (e.g., from trauma, mal-union fractures).
The purpose of the Ilizarov fixator is to stimulate bone growth by the principle of distraction osteogenesis.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission

claim.label: Post procedure X-ray showing implant labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) - affected part

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post procedure X ray show the implant?

common_queries: Post procedure X-ray does not show the implant.
Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission are missing or incomplete.
Radiological investigations confirming the diagnosis are missing or inconclusive.
Clinical photograph of affected part is missing.
Post procedure X-ray showing implant is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, and side (Left/Right).

clinical_key_pointers: Ilizarov Method: The Ilizarov apparatus is a set of external fixators consisting of rings, rods and kirschner wires, all made of stainless steel. It differs from the conventional external fixators in that it encases the limb as a cylinder, and it uses wires instead of pins to fix the bone to the rings.
The circular construction and tensioned wires allow early weight bearing as it provides far greater support than monolateral (one sided) fixators.
The purpose of the Ilizarov fixator is to stimulate bone growth, and this works by the principle of distraction osteogenesis, which is the pulling apart of bone to stimulate new bone growth.
